dossiers.bear_case: The parent kept the growth and shipped the legacy: revenue +1% total/+3% organic in 2025, perpetual licenses -33%, US revenue down three straight years, Q1 2026 constant-currency revenue below prior year, substantial maintenance revenue on legacy products with a dedicated AI-disruption risk factor. Margins deteriorated into the spin (GAAP op margin 24%->21%, net income -20%); 2025 GAAP flattered by a $55M contingent-consideration reversal; $137M/yr capitalized software flatters FCF. Q2 2026 will report a ~$481M non-cash trademark impairment on top of first-ever interest expense (~$25M/yr) with only 8% goodwill headroom on $6.22B in a single reporting unit. Controlled company: MSAB 42.9% votes on 21.8% economics, no hard lockup on Rule 144 sales; 'independent' chair is a sitting Koch executive on Hexagon's nomination committee; two unremediated material weaknesses; unquantified IRS NOPAs (2019-2020) with Octave owning pre-spin taxes; uncapped indemnities to Hexagon; near-zero purchased insider equity (54,021 shares group-wide). US federal-gov revenue engineered to transition back to Hexagon over time. Sections: Risk Factors, 10-Q MD&A/Note 14/Item 4.
dossiers.bull_case: Textbook forced-seller setup nearly complete: confirmed OMXS30 and FTSE All-World deletions already traded; window trigger fired on both tells (vol_ratio 0.353, price stabilized, day 24 of 90). What the sellers dumped is a real business — $1.64B revenue, two-thirds-plus recurring, 97% gross retention, 30%+ adjusted margins, $320M FCF at ~1.3x leverage. Anchor aligned: MSAB kept its entire pro-rata 58.4M shares (21.8% economics, 42.9% votes, 100% of super-voting Class A) and filed a 13D; registered exit gated to May 2027. Management is the division's own operators (CEO ran Hexagon ALI 2017-2025) with pay restructured into OCTV equity: $850K base (cut from $1.03M) vs ~$8.75M RSU/PSU spin grant, majority PSUs on spinco-only metrics (rTSR/ARR/margin) through 2028. Orphan status (only Nasdaq Composite) is the neglect fuel; eventual Russell/S&P inclusion is the designated forced-buyer exit. Sections: Business/Key Metrics, CD&A, 13D, 8-K Item 2.03.
dossiers.forced_seller_map: DONE: OMXS30 tracker funds and FTSE All-World passives — both deletions confirmed traded (volume spikes 5.4M on 6/10, 3.4M on 6/11, 4.7M on 6/18 vs ~0.8M recent avg; 17.1% of S/O traded on the US line in 24 sessions; -38% peak-to-trough to the 6/22 low $15.65, then stabilization). IN PROGRESS: benchmark-constrained Swedish/European active mandates (Swedbank Robur 6.7% of Class B is the archetype), softened because the Stockholm SDR line (~78% of shares initially) means holders are index-forced, not venue-forced; free SDR->US conversion ends ~Nov 2026, concentrating migration into H2 2026. MAY SELL LATER: MSCI/STOXX trackers if carried pending reclassification (UNCONFIRMED — residual risk); residual SDR holders force-sold at program termination ~2028; MSAB's 58.4M shares — constrained until May 2027 by RRA gating plus Rule 144 affiliate volume limits, with structural disincentive to sell Class A (dilutes 42.9% voting control); 13D Item 4 reserves the right to sell. MUST BUY: today only Nasdaq Composite trackers; Russell adds Sept/Dec 2026 if spin-off rules qualify (unverified), else June 2027 recon; S&P committee-dependent.
dossiers.key_risk: The forced-seller map may be incomplete: MSCI/STOXX treatment is unconfirmed, Stockholm SDR-line turnover is invisible to the US-line volume trigger that declared the window open, and the SDR free-conversion window ending ~Nov 2026 creates a slow-burn residual supply pipeline. If a residual European index deletion or SDR-driven supply wave lands on top of the pre-announced Q2 charge stack ($481M trademark impairment, first interest expense, material weaknesses), the name takes a second leg down with no confirmed forced buyer until Russell Sept/Dec 2026 at the earliest.
dossiers.post_spin_insider_activity: Form 4 sweep 2026-07-03 since distribution (2026-05-29): zero filings — structural, not behavioral: Octave is a foreign private issuer (Irish plc) and FPIs are Section 16-exempt, so insiders never file Form 4s. The channel is dark for this name; incentive_alignment rests on the Form 10 comp structure and the MSAB 13D, and the score already discounts for the missing visibility ('FPI status weakens insider-trade visibility' — rationale).
dossiers.pro_forma_notes: $1.64B revenue, 66-72% recurring, GRR 97% (99% large customers), NRR 105%, ARR $1,117M +8.7%, SaaS +25% in Q1 2026, adjusted operating margin 30-31%, FCF ~$320M. Debt: term loan $350M + EUR150M fully drawn plus ~$120M + EUR25M revolver draws (~$660M total) funding the $625M payment to Hexagon — ~1.3x adjusted operating income, ~2.0x GAAP operating income, ~1x covenant-basis EBITDA vs 3.50x covenant, ~$355M undrawn revolver. No DB pension (10-Q Note 8 DC-only), no material litigation (Note 11), no going-concern language. Thin cash: $80M pro forma plus $57M HQ sale-leaseback (10-Q Note 14).
